155620.explanation: A business-friendly climate is important for the (local) economy.

155621.explanation: Municipal Interests wants to focus on reducing regulatory pressure and creating a healthy business climate.

155626.explanation: This does not necessarily have to be on the company’s own premises.

155627.explanation: The replanting obligation should be maintained when issuing a felling permit.

155628.explanation: Providing information to residents to raise awareness about when burning is allowed and when it is not is important. The burning guide is the starting point.

155629.explanation: Agricultural land must remain agricultural land and should not be used for large-scale solar energy production.

155632.explanation: We would like to retain the current (temporary) cameras.

155633.explanation: Our own young people and elderly also want housing.

155634.explanation: The aim is 30% social rental housing. In some villages, more social rental homes are desirable, but it remains important to look at what is needed per village.

155635.explanation: This is up to the owner. Municipal Interests considers owner-occupation self-evident in new starter homes.

155638.explanation: To be filled in according to need.

155639.explanation: This also promotes traffic safety for all road users.

155640.explanation: This means that the housing corporation must first build new rental homes before selling elsewhere.

155641.explanation: We support innovative and alternative forms of housing (housing-care or protected housing forms).

155642.explanation: But new trees must be planted in return.

155643.explanation: This demand mainly comes from young people and elderly (single-person households).

155644.explanation: No more funding than what is provided by the national government.

155645.explanation: More is not appropriate for Lochem.

155647.explanation: It increases financial independence, encouraging people to leave social assistance.

155649.explanation: Everyone can contribute to society in their own way.

155650.explanation: We must organize this together with the surrounding municipalities. This can be a good step towards a regular job.

155651.explanation: It is important that the SME accountant supports them if necessary.

155652.explanation: The funds received for this should also be available for tourism and recreation.

155654.explanation: The Innovation Cooperative offers direct support for innovators in this sector.

155655.explanation: Young people must be more involved in future decisions; they are the future.

155656.explanation: The municipal council is already an elected body, chosen by the citizens.

155660.explanation: We want to strengthen tourism and recreation. Holiday parks can contribute to this.

155661.explanation: But fundamentally, we apply provincial and national policies.

155662.explanation: Some traditions should not be tampered with. Easter fires provide opportunities for meeting and connection.

155664.explanation: No money, there are various provincial and national schemes.

155665.explanation: It is good to investigate whether additional separation is cheaper.

155667.explanation: Information provision from the municipality is important alongside the national approach.

155668.explanation: Municipal Interests wants a predetermined safe place per town or neighborhood where people can go during emergencies, such as prolonged power outages.

155669.explanation: The Municipality lodged an objection in 2025. It is important that the defense forces can continue training.

155671.explanation: To maintain uniformity and safety, Municipal Interests believes the national traffic rules apply.

155672.explanation: Car sharing vehicles can park in regular parking spaces.

155675.explanation: We must ensure that response times are met.

155676.explanation: No facilities. However, it is important that the municipality continues to encourage businesses and organizations to join the 'High Emergency' app.

155677.explanation: A separate place for street art might be a good idea.

155681.explanation: Local associations are a binding factor in the communities.

155620.explanation: If Lochem remains an attractive location for businesses, this will increase employment in the municipality. GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem therefore supports a promotional campaign to retain companies in Lochem.

155621.explanation: Space is needed for companies that want to establish themselves in Lochem or want to expand. GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem wants to first utilize space in existing business parks. If expansion proves necessary, a new business park could be considered.

155626.explanation: Companies that want to house seasonal workers must arrange this themselves. GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem opposes housing on company premises, as it causes disorder in rural areas and increases employee isolation.

155627.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem protects our trees. Trees are defining elements of our pocket landscape and necessary links in biodiversity. For every tree cut down, a new tree must be planted, preferably more than one.

155628.explanation: Wood stoves are harmful to air quality, especially if used incorrectly or in the wrong weather conditions. This is bad for the environment and residents’ health. GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem tightens regulations around wood burning.

155629.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem prefers placing solar panels on roofs and unused land. If solar panels are placed on agricultural land, this will be done on a small scale or by exploring dual land use.

155632.explanation: Currently, camera surveillance in the station area is necessary. GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem is cautious about camera surveillance in public spaces but considers it useful here.

155633.explanation: For GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem, it is important that starting healthcare workers, trainee police officers, and beginning teachers find housing in Lochem. This allows people in crucial professions to live in the neighborhoods and villages where they serve.

155634.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem advocates for 35% social rental homes and 40% affordable owner-occupied homes to get the housing market moving again. At the moment, the percentage of social rental homes in Lochem is far too low.

155635.explanation: With an owner-occupancy obligation, we make the housing market more accessible for our residents, especially starters. We prevent investors from massively buying cheap and mid-priced homes to rent out and driving up rents. Hence, GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem supports an owner-occupancy requirement.

155638.explanation: An increasing number of car owners drive electric, so charging opportunities in Lochem need to increase as well. Public charging points for electric cars are needed. GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem therefore supports designating specific parking spaces with charging facilities.

155639.explanation: Within the built-up area of the villages and the city of Lochem, speeding is common, such as on Barchemseweg in Lochem. To improve traffic safety, GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem wants to introduce a maximum speed of 30 km/h everywhere.

155640.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem wants to preserve social rental homes for their intended purpose. Lochem currently has too few social rental homes, so we want to maintain the restriction against buying these homes.

155641.explanation: There are countless good ways to have elderly people live together and be cared for. To enable these living arrangements, municipal coordination is necessary.

155642.explanation: We must protect the trees and nature in our municipality. In new construction, cutting down a single tree may be necessary, but replanting will follow. GroenLinks-PvdA opposes large-scale cutting for housing development, such as in the Barchem forest.

155643.explanation: To give starters a chance on the housing market and enable flow, cheap rental and owner-occupied apartments must be built. GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem supports 35% social rental homes and 40% affordable owner-occupied to revitalize the housing market.

155644.explanation: Temporary housing is a good plan to quickly build homes for refugees with residence permits. These temporary neighborhoods should also accommodate other groups, such as urgent seekers, youth, and elderly.

155645.explanation: It has been agreed that the Asylum Seeker Center on Ampsenseweg will house 250 people. We adhere to this. If there is a request to accommodate more asylum seekers in our municipality, GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem is happy to consider options – but at a different location than Ampsenseweg.

155647.explanation: Work must always pay off. GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem encourages people to work and fights the poverty trap by fostering trust, which increases the chance of sustainable employment.

155649.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem wants to help people get into work as much as possible. Sheltered jobs can be a step towards regular employment. They provide daily structure and increase self-esteem.

155650.explanation: Every talent counts. Vulnerable residents cannot always work at regular companies and need guidance. There must always be a permanent place for these people at a social workplace. GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem believes these should be development companies with good coaching and training.

155651.explanation: Innovative ideas are needed to advance our municipality and keep our economy flexible and modern. GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem supports this fund and finds it important that innovation also focuses on a sustainable society and social issues.

155652.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem does not want the money collected from tourist tax to be locked in this way. The revenues may be used more broadly.

155654.explanation: For the quality of the landscape, healthy food, and the future of farmers, it is important that farmers can transition to organic farming. GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem wants the municipality to support this where possible and is willing to contribute.

155655.explanation: The voice of young people is very important and is not always clearly heard in the town hall. Young people have many refreshing and innovative perspectives. GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem sees a youth council as a great way to enable this.

155656.explanation: Citizen assemblies have proven successful in various municipalities and nationally. They bring together residents from diverse backgrounds around an issue, yielding beautiful and unexpected solutions. GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem wants the municipality to explore how to deploy a citizen assembly.

155660.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem does not want to allow new holiday parks. We must be careful with our green countryside. Additional holiday parks cause more clutter, light pollution, and traffic movements.

155661.explanation: The healthy living environment of our residents is paramount. (Agricultural) chemicals are harmful to people, animals, and nature and therefore do not belong in the living environment of people and animals. That is why ample buffer zones around schools, homes, and nature areas are necessary.

155662.explanation: The Easter fires in the municipality of Lochem are small-scale and organized by volunteers. GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem wants to preserve this tradition. There is no room for new Easter fires.

155664.explanation: Many farmers and citizens roll up their sleeves for the landscape and nature. Often they do this voluntarily. Through this effort, ecological connections are strengthened and Lochem becomes more beautiful. All initiatives that enhance nature and biodiversity are supported by GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem.

155665.explanation: Research shows that 'post-sorting' is a better way to separate waste. The existing system makes residents more aware of their responsibility to separate waste, but in practice many (plastic) containers are heavily contaminated.

155667.explanation: Digital scams or other online dangers can cause serious problems for residents. Therefore, protection is important and necessary.

155668.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem wants all villages and neighborhoods to be prepared for an emergency. Emergency power supply is part of that.

155669.explanation: Het Groote Veld is one of the larger, contiguous nature reserves in our area. Low-flying helicopters disrupt nature. Therefore, the municipality must object to exercises involving low-flying helicopters.

155671.explanation: It must be attractive to take the bike. Therefore, good cycling routes are needed. But bicycle safety in our municipality also needs to be improved. 'Always priority for cyclists' provides clarity in traffic and therefore GroenLinks-PvdA wants to always give cyclists priority over cars within the built-up area.

155672.explanation: More and more people feel part of the sharing economy and use shared cars. By giving them fixed spots in the city or villages, there is always parking space for the shared car and awareness is increased.

155673.explanation: Trees that pose a danger must be cut down. The safety of road users is paramount. GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem wants to cut down only trees that pose an immediate danger and replanting is always mandatory.

155675.explanation: Quick access to emergency care is very important for all residents. The municipality does not control the station locations, but response times in our municipality must improve. GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem will fully cooperate if the Regional Ambulance Service indicates that a post is needed.

155676.explanation: The municipality of Lochem has hardly any public toilets. In urgent situations, this is very inconvenient. GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem wants more public toilets and better visibility of the existing ones.

155677.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem supports street artists and is happy with artworks, such as on the silo at Goorseweg. Designating a legal place for graffiti will not stop nuisance; other measures are needed for that.

155681.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Lochem wants more space and attention for art and culture. The foundation for this lies with the dozens of music, theater, and other creative associations in our municipality. The municipality must be generous so these associations can continue their work.

155620.explanation: It is good to let people who do not know the municipality of Lochem get to know what we have to offer to both entrepreneurs and residents. Entrepreneurs should be given space: fast permits, fewer rules, and no increase in costs. Practically feasible, affordable, and with local support.

155621.explanation: Each center must have sufficient stock of business parks to enable existing entrepreneurs to grow and offer space to new entrepreneurs with as few rules as possible that are locally or socially tied to that center. Entrepreneurs must be given space: fast permits, fewer rules.

155626.explanation: No obligation, if possible fine. We mainly build on the edges of the centers, with affordable owner-occupied homes and smart housing forms for starters and seniors. Suitable market initiatives that focus on quality housing for labor migrants are also honored.

155627.explanation: Of course, there may be more of these. This also contributes to livability, sustainability, and the climate objectives.

155628.explanation: We trust responsible use by our residents. However, it is advised to follow the stove guide. Practically feasible, affordable, and with local support.

155629.explanation: Agricultural land is already scarce and we need it for many other tasks such as extensive livestock farming, housing construction, energy transition, water storage, and nature. Practically feasible, affordable, and with local support.

155632.explanation: Not only in the station area but in more places mobile cameras can be placed if there is cause due to nuisance and vandalism. Safety is a core task: better lighting, visible enforcement, and where necessary camera surveillance at risk locations.

155633.explanation: We ensure that there are homes for every target group. Every job and role in a society contributes to this and is valuable. Practically feasible and with local support.

155634.explanation: Not a dogma. We follow national legislation that must be complied with and look at the entire municipality because needs may differ per center. We mainly build on the edges of the centers, with affordable owner-occupied homes and smart housing forms for starters and seniors.

155635.explanation: It is perfectly fine to buy a home for a child or parent.

155638.explanation: The current locations are often still unused. We want to follow the pace of electrification of car ownership but no more. Especially considering grid congestion, homes and businesses have priority now. We choose solutions that work: practical, affordable, and with room for initiative.

155639.explanation: 30 km/h is fine in a neighborhood. On the more through roads within the built-up area 50 km/h is fine; villages should remain accessible and attractive for public transport. We build on the edges of the centers, with affordable owner-occupied homes and smart housing forms for starters and seniors.

155640.explanation: That is only allowed if the total social rental housing stock does not decrease, there is a surplus (more social rental homes than demand for this type of housing), or if a corporation wants to sell part of the existing stock by building the same number of new homes in that center to be able to make them more sustainable.

155641.explanation: Developers and initiators should be invited to offer suitable solutions for these housing forms. Care must be close by: support for informal caregivers and prevention in youth care, with attention to cohesion in the villages. We choose smart housing forms for seniors.

155642.explanation: These must be compensated as much as possible elsewhere and in the new neighborhoods sufficient green must be incorporated. This is sustainable and prevents heat stress. We mainly build on the edges of the centers, with affordable owner-occupied homes and smart housing forms for starters and seniors.

155643.explanation: We follow the demand in the housing market. The municipality does not dictate where people must live. However, we challenge developers to come up with affordable and smart housing forms for starters and seniors.

155644.explanation: Flexible housing as a transitional step fits well, but not only for refugees, also for young people and seniors. We mainly build on the edges of the centers, with affordable owner-occupied homes and smart housing forms for starters and seniors.

155645.explanation: We have also agreed on this long-term in our municipality. Safety is a core task: better lighting, better traffic safety, visible enforcement, and where necessary camera surveillance at risk locations.

155647.explanation: But work must be the starting point and it must pay off, meaning it should provide an extra benefit on top of the benefit. We choose solutions that work: practical, affordable, and with room for initiative.

155649.explanation: First, the reasons why people are long-term unemployed must be well understood, but suitable (social) work is a good starting point. Entrepreneurs must be given space: fast permits, fewer rules, and no increase in local taxes, and they can also help long-term unemployed find work.

155650.explanation: If we can do this together in a broader region, that is also fine.

155651.explanation: To challenge entrepreneurs to apply innovations in sustainability and technology, an innovation fund could stimulate this. Entrepreneurs must be given space: fast permits, fewer rules, and no increase in local taxes.

155652.explanation: This money is also paid by tourists to keep this area attractive for recreation in the future and to enjoy cultural offerings. Entrepreneurs must be given space: fast permits, fewer rules, and no increase in local taxes.

155654.explanation: It is an economic principle of supply and demand. A revenue model must come from the market. Entrepreneurs must be given space: fast permits, fewer rules, and no increase in local taxes.

155655.explanation: This way you also involve young people more in decision-making and they can also bring up topics that specifically concern them and ask for attention. We choose solutions that work: practical, affordable, and with room for initiative.

155656.explanation: We have a council and a high degree of participation. In the centers, we also have good contact with the village councils which are very active there and with whom we are happy to cooperate. We choose solutions that work: practical, affordable, and with room for initiative.

155660.explanation: Holiday parks are allowed if there is market demand and the impact on the surroundings is manageable. We choose solutions that work: practical, affordable, and with room for personal initiative.

155661.explanation: We follow national laws and regulations, and if suitable and feasible alternatives (such as precision agriculture) arise that improve quality of life and health, we will support them. We choose solutions that work: practical, affordable, and with room for personal initiative.

155662.explanation: It is a cultural heritage that we certainly support, but it must fit within the legal frameworks. We choose solutions that work: practical, affordable, and simple.

155664.explanation: There are already other Gelderland, national, and European compensations for that. We choose feasible, affordable measures: especially solar panels on roofs, local storage, precision agriculture using GPS and AI, and solutions with broad support.

155665.explanation: Unless there is a suitable environmentally conscious and financially feasible alternative, we are happy to cooperate. We choose solutions that work: practical, affordable, and with room for personal initiative.

155667.explanation: Helping by providing information. We choose solutions that work: practical, affordable, and with room for personal initiative.

155668.explanation: This is important for essential functions in emergencies, especially given current geopolitical developments and to reduce grid congestion. It increases self-reliance and safety.

155669.explanation: Defense also requires training for our security. We choose solutions that work: practical, affordable, and with room for personal initiative.

155671.explanation: Simply complying with the applicable traffic rules. We choose solutions that work: practically feasible, affordable, and with local support.

155672.explanation: If it appears that there is great demand and the current locations are insufficient, parking spaces can be converted into shared car spots. We choose solutions that work: practical, affordable, and with room for personal initiative.

155673.explanation: Safety comes first; however, we believe these must be compensated.

155675.explanation: Every minute counts, and response times in our municipality are far too long. We choose solutions that work: practical, affordable, and with room for personal initiative.

155676.explanation: There are also alternatives such as hospitality venues, and entrepreneurs and institutions that make their toilets publicly available. We choose solutions that work: practical, affordable, and with room for personal initiative.

155677.explanation: This is more a task for youth centers and other creative venues. We choose solutions that work: practical, affordable, and with room for personal initiative.

155681.explanation: Music has a positive effect on the well-being and health of people; it is educational and provides social connection. We invest in sports and social gatherings in all communities so that clubs remain strong and everyone can participate.

155620.explanation: MmL believes that much promotion can be made for tourism in the Lochem municipality: this is a very important industry within the municipality involving many SMEs. 'Clean' industry is always welcome in our municipality.

155621.explanation: Recently, Diekink was added as an industrial area for the city of Lochem; plots are still being sold at Stijgoord. All population centers have industrial areas of varying sizes. MmL has not received signals that there is currently a need for more or expanded industrial sites.

155626.explanation: This is entirely up to the employer: like in other sectors, they decide whether to provide housing. Naturally, to build on private property it must fit within the spatial planning and a permit must be granted.

155627.explanation: The replanting obligation has existed for years in Lochem: for every tree cut down, a new tree must be planted within 3 years. To encourage tree planting, the municipality annually gives hundreds of trees to residents under the motto 'A tree must be able to'.

155628.explanation: Wood burning is also a widely used heating method in the Achterhoek. The Burning Guide can be consulted to see if there are conditions (for example fog) that advise against burning out of consideration for neighbors.

155629.explanation: The province of Gelderland established in 2024 a policy banning solar fields on prime agricultural land. All municipalities in Gelderland are required to implement this policy.

155632.explanation: In mid-2025, there was an incident that received considerable media attention. Partly for that reason, a temporary camera was installed. No other noteworthy incidents occurred before or after placement: for MmL, this is reason to stop using the camera after the pilot.

155633.explanation: Housing corporations determine, based on their policy, who receives social rental housing. Today, many professions face shortages: differentiating by profession would lead to discrimination.

155634.explanation: It would be nice if Lochem had more social housing, but young residents in our municipality often want to buy homes. That would be made impossible by this. MmL therefore advocates that large-scale building projects include both affordable rental and owner-occupied homes, together accounting for 35%.

155635.explanation: Lochem has had a self-occupation policy for years: the buyer must live in the newly built home for at least 5 years before selling or renting it out.

155638.explanation: There are already many charging points within Lochem. The municipality, together with the province, has granted a concession to Vattenfall: this company will provide around 300 charging stations across the municipality in the coming years.

155639.explanation: It is national policy that roads within built-up areas have a 30 km/h limit. In the interest of traffic safety, MmL also supports this. Only where 50 km/h is safe can that limit remain.

155640.explanation: The houses are owned by the corporations: they may decide whether to sell their homes and to whom. It is fine if a tenant can purchase their rental home, but the municipality has no role in this.

155641.explanation: Although MmL recognizes a certain need for this type of housing, it is not the municipality’s responsibility to invest money in it. Naturally, the municipality can stimulate development, for example through selling municipal land.

155642.explanation: If there is no alternative, a tree may be cut down to build homes. This is an exception if the tree is a landmark tree: such trees may not be cut down for this purpose. Fortunately, existing trees can often be well integrated into building plans.

155643.explanation: There is a clear need in Lochem for smaller and thus more affordable homes. It has been decided that 35% of homes in building plans must be affordable.

155644.explanation: Municipalities do not build homes; however, for housing, primarily for Ukrainians, the council decided otherwise by permitting temporary housing through homes built on the municipality’s order. Financing was based on subsidies received from the national government.

155645.explanation: The Lochem council decided this in 2025: residents and neighbors can rely on this.

155647.explanation: Legally, people with social assistance benefits may earn up to €219 extra per month for a maximum of 6 months. The intent is to lead to a permanent job. Any amount earned above this during the period is deducted from benefits.

155649.explanation: Het Baken, part of the municipality, works with Circulus and also with the Employer Service Point FactorWerk to help guide the long-term unemployed toward work.

155650.explanation: Together with Circulus, the municipality offers residents sheltered learning and experience places, with the possibility of obtaining permanent employment.

155651.explanation: The municipality has had an Innovation Fund for years: entrepreneurs with sustainable innovation ideas can apply to this fund.

155652.explanation: This is a wish MmL has had for a long time: with it, the municipality can increase Lochem’s tourist appeal, ensuring tourists get value for their tax money.

155654.explanation: The choice is up to the farmer: there are national schemes in this area. In MmL’s opinion, this is not a task for the municipality.

155655.explanation: The municipal council decides on behalf of all residents, including youth. When an important decision affects young people, they are often involved beforehand. Several years ago, an initiative was taken to establish a youth council; it was not successful at the time.

155656.explanation: The democratically elected municipal council decides and advises the executive board on behalf of the residents. There must be a very important reason to also assemble a citizens' assembly. This assembly must then answer a specific question. In any case, the municipal council remains the highest authority.

155660.explanation: Initiators can decide for themselves whether they want to build a holiday park: of course, they must comply with existing regulations. Their plans must meet the requirements of the zoning plan.

155661.explanation: Although there are various opinions about these substances, municipalities do not control them: the CTGB (the Board for the Authorization of Plant Protection Products and Biocides), appointed by the national government, determines what is permitted and how it should be used. Municipalities have no authority over this.

155662.explanation: Easter fires are part of our traditions: naturally, safety is paramount. For this reason, they can be prohibited to prevent fire spread if there has been a period of severe dryness.

155664.explanation: MmL strongly supports nature conservation and development. Nevertheless, we believe that not everything should be subsidized, especially because there is much debate about what constitutes improvement in this regard.

155665.explanation: The current system encourages residents to properly separate their waste. Many products (PMD, paper, textiles, etc.) can be disposed of free of charge. Removing this incentive would quickly bring us back to square one. MmL would like research into whether post-sorting is cheaper and more effective.

155667.explanation: It is good if the municipality also informs residents about these forms of digital crime through its regular channels (messages in the BerkelBode and the municipal website).

155668.explanation: The electricity supply is structurally 99.9% reliable: placing diesel generators in many locations now is not only totally unnecessary but also very costly. Furthermore, energy supply is a provincial responsibility, not a municipal one.

155669.explanation: The municipality has timely expressed its concerns regarding plans for low-flying helicopters. At the same time, MmL understands that the Defense Department needs places for training. The plans were finalized by the cabinet in December 2025: objections are no longer possible.

155671.explanation: Cyclists often already have priority based on traffic laws: only if a car is driving on a priority road and the cyclist is not, does the car have priority. We want to keep the current rules.

155672.explanation: There are plans for more so-called Mobility Hubs within our municipality where shared cars (often from Lochem Energie) can be parked: if a neighborhood jointly purchases and uses a car, MmL finds it logical that a fixed parking spot in the neighborhood is designated.

155673.explanation: If there truly is no other solution (for example, by pruning), then a tree must be allowed to be cut down, naturally with a replanting obligation.

155675.explanation: MmL has serious concerns about ambulance response times from Zutphen, Apeldoorn, and Deventer. For this reason, we strongly support establishing a permanent ambulance station in the city of Lochem so that our residents receive timely care in emergencies.

155676.explanation: MmL has wanted for years that free public toilets be available in the city of Lochem as well as in the villages, ideally accessible 24 hours a day.

155677.explanation: Spraying graffiti on someone else's property is prohibited and, as far as MmL is concerned, that should remain so: you may not damage others, even if it is so-called artistic expression.

155681.explanation: Various cultural associations currently receive thousands of euros in subsidies per year. We have not heard any calls for increasing subsidies.

155620.explanation: The municipality of Lochem does not need to profile itself as a location for as many companies as possible. That puts pressure on space, nature, and livability. LochemGroen! wants to attract companies that contribute to the municipality: sustainable, innovative, and social. The focus is on quality over quantity.

155621.explanation: Space in Lochem is limited. Therefore, we oppose new industrial estates, such as the planned site at Diekink, where valuable green areas are under pressure. LochemGroen! opts to preserve nature and landscape and to make existing industrial sites more sustainable and better utilized.

155626.explanation: Combining living and working on one company’s premises makes employees vulnerable and increases the risk of exploitation. LochemGroen! favors agriculture with less seasonal labor and more local, nature-inclusive businesses, with fair working conditions and good housing, independent of the employer.

155627.explanation: Tree felling must be avoided as much as possible. Old trees are crucial for nature, climate, and livability and cannot be replaced one-to-one. If felling is necessary, for example due to disease, extensive replanting must follow with multiple new trees, ensuring a net increase in greenery in the municipality of Lochem.

155628.explanation: Health and quality of life come first. Wood burning can cause nuisance and health problems in unfavorable weather. Therefore, LochemGroen! wants the Burning Guide to be strictly followed, with good information and enforcement. Residents are encouraged to use clean, sustainable heating methods.

155629.explanation: Agricultural land is scarce and intended for food, nature, and landscape. Therefore, LochemGroen! opposes large-scale solar panels on agricultural land. Solar panels belong on roofs and paved areas. Only in exceptional cases, under strict conditions, is small-scale generation allowed on land with little natural value.

155632.explanation: Camera surveillance is not a goal in itself, but due to persistent vandalism, intervention was necessary. After pressure from LochemGroen!, cameras were installed at the station in 2025. This is temporary until the ring road is completed and the station area is lively and safe again for residents and travelers.

155633.explanation: Essential professions such as teachers, caregivers, and police officers with local ties must be able to live in Lochem. Due to aging, livability is under pressure. By giving priority to these professions in housing within regulations, care, education, and safety in Lochem continue to function well.

155634.explanation: Everyone has the right to affordable housing. In Lochem, the share of social rental housing is too low, and the shortage is great. LochemGroen! wants at least 35% social rental housing in new developments. Affordable homeownership is also needed to keep villages livable and services future-proof.

155635.explanation: Speculation with homes drives up prices and increases the housing shortage. Housing is not an investment product but a basic need. Therefore, LochemGroen! wants owner-occupancy obligations so that buyers live in their homes. With a self-occupancy requirement and anti-speculation clause, we keep homes affordable now and in the future.

155638.explanation: LochemGroen! wants to promote sustainable mobility. More charging points are needed to enable electric driving and shared cars. Together with key partner LochemEnergie, we want to make electric driving accessible to everyone and encourage fewer cars and more shared mobility in residential areas.

155639.explanation: Lower speeds within built-up areas lead to more traffic safety, less nuisance, and a more pleasant living environment. It encourages walking and cycling and makes neighborhoods safer for children and the elderly. LochemGroen! has advocated this for years; now it is time to structurally implement 30 km/h zones.

155640.explanation: Social rental homes are built with public funds to enable affordable housing. Selling them reduces the stock and increases the housing shortage unless social rentals are added elsewhere. We choose to protect social rental housing: housing is a right, not a profit model.

155641.explanation: Lochem is among the most aging municipalities, and care is under pressure. We want to invest significantly in new living forms where care, housing, and social interaction come together. Creative care housing initiatives, such as young and old living together, strengthen mutual support and improve quality of life.

155642.explanation: Trees are highly valuable for climate, nature, and quality of life. Housing developments must take this into account from the start. There are sufficient opportunities to build without cutting trees or building in stream valleys. This way, we choose housing balanced with nature and landscape.

155643.explanation: Small rental and owner-occupied apartments meet the needs of youth, starters, and seniors. In Lochem, we live more spaciously while space is limited. By building compactly, better using vacancies, and allowing for extensions and room rentals, we keep housing affordable.

155644.explanation: Due to the housing shortage, temporary accommodation for status holders is sometimes necessary. The municipality must take responsibility with temporary housing forms that later remain available to residents. At the same time, sufficient affordable housing is needed so that emergency shelter becomes unnecessary over time.

155645.explanation: No one flees war or violence voluntarily. Municipalities must take responsibility for people seeking protection. The agreement of 250 shelter places is a good starting point but not a hard maximum. Additional shelter should be negotiable, with attention to good integration and coexistence.

155647.explanation: Those who earn extra income while on welfare show initiative to improve their situation. Immediate cuts are counterproductive. Research shows that trust works better than strict rules. Therefore, we want to allow space for temporarily earning part-time income so people gain work experience and can transition sustainably.

155649.explanation: Not everyone finds a job in the regular labor market, but everyone deserves to participate. Therefore, the municipality must offer sheltered jobs. It is not only about work but about involvement, being seen, and becoming part of society again. This way, no one is left behind.

155650.explanation: Not everyone finds suitable work on the regular labor market. A social workplace offers people with disabilities structure, income, and self-worth. Initiatives such as the bicycle repair shop in Eefde show that this takes time. Therefore, we look beyond costs alone.

155651.explanation: A business fund is only sensible if innovation contributes to social and ecological goals. LochemGroen! wants to invest in pioneering entrepreneurs working on sustainability, livability, and the local community - not in profit-driven projects without public value.

155652.explanation: Tourist tax is not a subsidy fund for one sector but a contribution to public spaces. We support sustainable recreation, but the proceeds must also be used for nature, the living environment, and facilities that absorb the pressure of tourism.

155654.explanation: Farmers have long been steered toward intensive production. If we want the transition to organic and nature-inclusive agriculture, support is needed. We want to deploy a transition fund to help farmers make the change. This way, we invest in healthy soil, clean water, and biodiversity.

155655.explanation: Young people bring new ideas and energy. With a youth council, they get a fixed place within the municipality. This increases engagement, strengthens democratic values, and ensures that policies better align with the lives of young people. This way, we invest in trust and the future of Lochem.

155656.explanation: Citizen participation is important and takes many forms. A citizens' assembly can be valuable for large, complex issues, but it is not a standard tool. It can supplement the municipal council, not replace it, and input must also remain possible through other accessible forms.

155660.explanation: The strength of our municipality lies in peace, nature, and landscape. New holiday parks put this under pressure and cause clutter. Therefore, we choose to protect nature and the living environment. Recreation remains important, with a focus on existing initiatives that fit with liveability.

155661.explanation: People and nature must be protected. As long as the national government does not ban agricultural pesticides, the municipality must intervene itself. LochemGroen! took the lead by banning non-organic flower cultivation and combines strict rules with support for farmers switching to pesticide-free farming.

155662.explanation: Easter fires pose risks to safety, air quality, and biodiversity. Therefore, there are better ways to celebrate spring. In 2025, LochemGroen! introduced the idea of creating brushwood heaps instead of Easter fires. These provide shelters, enhance biodiversity, and bring people together.

155664.explanation: Hedges, hedgerows, and tree lines are indispensable for biodiversity and form a 'highway' for fauna. The municipality must support improving this nature. With subsidies and a single clear desk, we make it easier to strengthen the landscape together and create living space for animals.

155665.explanation: Separating waste is important for reuse. Paying per container can help but is not a goal in itself. If research shows that sorting waste afterwards is more environmentally friendly and fairer, we want to abolish this system. We choose whatever works best for people and the environment.

155667.explanation: Residents must be able to protect themselves against digital fraud. The municipality can help with this, together with the national government, by providing good information and support. This requires an easily accessible desk where reports are taken seriously and followed up promptly, so residents can count on help and trust.

155668.explanation: Attention to power outages is needed. The safety region must take the lead in this. We focus on awareness, clear emergency plans, and good communication, together with the safety region, network operators, and emergency services. Whether emergency power is needed and effective in each village requires tailor-made solutions.

155669.explanation: Low-flying military helicopters harm nature, living environment, and safety. Therefore, the municipality must lodge objections. The Groote Veld is a vulnerable natural area. We are not against Defence but support careful choices. LochemGroen! was the only party to submit an official opinion.

155671.explanation: In villages and neighborhoods, cyclists and pedestrians must be central. By giving cyclists priority and treating cars as guests, it becomes safer and more pleasant. We want streets to be more than asphalt, like greening Nieuwstad (Lochem) - a bicycle street for a livable, healthy environment.

155672.explanation: Shared cars make it possible to stay mobile with fewer cars. By designating more spots for shared cars, sharing becomes more attractive than ownership. We want to make shared mobility affordable and accessible, also for people on lower incomes.

155673.explanation: Healthy trees do not pose a danger. Traffic safety is related to speed, driving behavior, and road design. Trees should not be cut down for convenience; first, we look at measures such as speed reduction or road adjustments. Trees contribute to liveability-nature-and deserve protection.

155675.explanation: Good ambulance care is life-saving. Locations are determined regionally based on response times and fall under the safety region. Having a dedicated location is very costly. Therefore, we focus on better regional coordination and faster response times so that residents receive help quickly.

155676.explanation: Public toilets are essential to participate in society. Without facilities, people stay home or avoid the center. We want more accessible toilets in the municipality. Apps like the Hoge Nood app help, but the municipality remains responsible for the facilities.

155677.explanation: By creating legal places for graffiti and street art, creativity gets space and illegal nuisance decreases. We want to challenge young people to show their talent and give street art value in public spaces, while the municipality continues to act against graffiti in unwanted places.

155681.explanation: Local music and theater associations are very valuable for culture, meeting, and cohesion in our municipalities and deserve support. Situations like that of music association Advendo show that targeted support is needed to keep associations vital, accessible, and future-proof.

155620.explanation: D66 wants the centers of our municipality to remain at least as lively as they are. Where 'holes' occur due to relocating or closing businesses, we want new companies quickly on those empty spots. That way, we remain the attractive municipality we are.

155621.explanation: There is currently almost no space for companies to expand. Successful entrepreneurship often means moving away now. D66 wants successful companies to be able to do business within the municipality.

155626.explanation: When employers bring seasonal workers here, they have a responsibility for their housing. On their own premises is not always appropriate or a good solution, for example due to effects on local residents.

155628.explanation: We live in a rural municipality. Banning the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves goes too far. However, it is wise for Lochem to consider whether this is a good idea in all neighborhoods and under all weather conditions.

155629.explanation: The municipality has strict policies about where new solar panels can be installed. And rightly so. But we cannot prohibit existing solar panels on agricultural land.

155632.explanation: There is already camera surveillance. Images can only be reviewed afterward, following an incident. D66 weighs combating insecurity, nuisance, and vandalism against privacy concerns. In this case, it is a good idea for the cameras to remain.

155633.explanation: D66 wants our own residents, such as young people who grew up in one of our villages and want to stay there, to have priority when assigning rental homes. The municipality must cooperate with housing corporations and project developers to achieve this.

155634.explanation: D66 wants building to match existing demand. Affordable housing must be provided but that can also be cheap homeownership instead of social rental housing. For this, the municipality must develop a housing vision together with residents. Then we can decide together where social rental and ownership will be located.

155635.explanation: Speculation with homes makes houses more expensive and rents unaffordable. D66 is against that.

155638.explanation: The number of electric cars in the municipality is increasing. D66 welcomes this. Too few charging spots should not be an obstacle to buying an electric car.

155639.explanation: There are some main routes in the municipality where 30 km/h is inappropriate, but in general, 30 km/h within built-up areas is a good idea.

155640.explanation: People buying their own home invest in their future and build capital. Those who want and can do this should reasonably be able to.

155641.explanation: D66 supports new forms of living and backs initiatives by older residents who want to live together to arrange their care collectively.

155642.explanation: It may be that trees are cut down or nature is affected for building new houses. D66 believes that in such cases, the trees and nature must be compensated for generously.

155643.explanation: The average home in the municipality of Lochem is large compared to the rest of the Netherlands. There is great need for affordable housing, including smaller rental and owner-occupied apartments.

155644.explanation: D66 is in favor of flexible housing for refugees and other urgent seekers, whether they come from abroad or from our own municipality. The flexible homes built in recent years in Lochem and Gorssel have been a success!

155645.explanation: For the current shelter location, it was agreed with nearby residents that no more than 250 people would be there. D66 considers this agreement important. If the municipality ever has to designate a new location elsewhere, we will make new agreements.

155649.explanation: The municipality helps people who do not fully participate in society and, for example, are unemployed. This can have many different causes due to various personal circumstances. D66 stands for tailor-made solutions, as the municipality currently provides.

155650.explanation: The municipality of Lochem currently has no own sheltered employment. Local entrepreneurs offer places to people who deserve sheltered jobs, and they do this well. D66 believes that, in addition, a dedicated facility is needed.

155651.explanation: There is much creativity and entrepreneurship in Lochem. D66 wants to encourage that.

155652.explanation: D66 believes the municipality should invest well in recreational facilities. But the tourist tax is also for the maintenance of municipal facilities-from roads and public greenery to the theater.

155654.explanation: Farmers who want to switch to organic farming must be able to get help. D66 believes the municipality should assist them with advice and action. Knowledge and contacts are often even more important than just giving money.

155655.explanation: Young people deserve a voice. The Municipality of Lochem must organize a youth council to better hear that voice.

155660.explanation: Tourism is important for Lochem. D66 sees no room for new large holiday parks. Small parks focused on sustainable tourism can be a good addition. Where such a park is located and how it is integrated is very important, because not everything can be placed everywhere.

155664.explanation: D66 believes the municipality must develop a vision on nature management and development. In places where the municipality wants to strengthen nature, it makes sense for the municipality to invest there together with farmers and residents.

155665.explanation: The system causes too much residual waste to end up with plastic waste and waste being dumped where it shouldn't be. D66 thinks the municipality should simultaneously introduce a sorting system to recover reusable raw materials.

155667.explanation: D66 believes that municipal digital services must be secure and easily accessible. The municipality cannot interfere with everything people do online.

155668.explanation: D66 sees a resilient society as very important. Good emergency power supplies are part of that, for the entire municipality. We must arrange that together with the whole region, not per village.

155669.explanation: Last year, the municipality objected together with other Achterhoek municipalities. Nevertheless, the cabinet adopted the National Spatial Plan for Defense. That plan can no longer be contested or appealed.

155673.explanation: D66 believes that trees dangerous to traffic must be cut down and compensated with new planting in a safe location.

155675.explanation: Ambulance response times are not adequate in many areas of the municipality. D66 thinks this must improve. That can be done with its own base or a shared base with a neighboring municipality.

155620.explanation: Besides regional employment, the municipality of Lochem also needs local employment. Generally, employees prefer to live closer to work to improve work-life balance. It is also an advantage for people without their own transport.

155621.explanation: CDA believes that the Diekink business park along the canal must be developed to stimulate local employment. Available spots on existing industrial estates Aalsvoort and Stijgoord should also be utilized.

155626.explanation: CDA believes entrepreneurs are themselves responsible for the activities they undertake. If they employ workers from other regions or countries, proper housing complying with all legal regulations must be provided. The government must actively monitor this.

155627.explanation: CDA wants more trees in the municipality of Lochem. Besides replanting, the municipality must take more initiative to plant new trees. This can be done independently by the municipality or through actions with residents.

155628.explanation: Burning fireplaces and stoves should remain possible but limited to periods when, according to the KNMI, it does not cause nuisance to the neighborhood. CDA believes that the municipality of Lochem should follow the RIVM’s burning guide.

155629.explanation: CDA wants agricultural lands to remain available for agricultural activities. Ground-based livestock farming becomes more important in making the sector sustainable. This requires availability of land for the sector.

155632.explanation: Lochem station is a notorious location. Many incidents have taken place there. It is time for the municipality to listen and act on residents' reports.

155633.explanation: More Dutch people are searching for a home. Giving priority to a specific professional group does not change the housing supply. The solution is to build, not to grant priority to a small number of professions.

155634.explanation: Social rental housing is important for people with a smaller budget. Rental homes are often the first step for young people to live independently. CDA believes that by providing more rental housing, we ensure solidarity and a balanced age distribution within our municipality.

155635.explanation: Many private landlords buy houses to make a profit. The profit portion among those landlords drives up housing costs for our residents. Social rental housing and home ownership make housing affordable.

155638.explanation: If electric driving is nationally encouraged, charging points must also be easily available. The number should become so large that it no longer matters whether an electric or fuel car is parked in that spot.

155639.explanation: CDA believes that for traffic safety, a maximum speed of 30 km/h is necessary within built-up areas. This excludes main roads that carry traffic through town centers.

155640.explanation: Selling rental homes should stimulate housing corporations to build more homes again. It is a possibility to expand the housing supply. At the same time, it must not lead to fewer social rental homes, so corporations must continue building.

155641.explanation: The society is aging and CDA believes we must prepare by building living environments where elderly people can live comfortably.

155642.explanation: Construction must not be stopped by the presence of trees. Replanting (or preservation) of trees should be part of housing plans. The number of trees should remain the same or preferably increase in the area around the housing project.

155643.explanation: Smaller homes provide a starting opportunity for young people. In the future, there will be more single-person households and the market must adapt to that.

155644.explanation: The municipality of Lochem already contributes by establishing an asylum seeker center (AZC) at Ampsen, along with other reception locations in the municipality. This meets the national standard set at the country level. Municipalities below the standard must first contribute.

155645.explanation: The location must always fit within the possibilities of the environment. Larger centers put more pressure on the surroundings.

155647.explanation: The goal is to leave social assistance and to stand on one’s own feet. Gradual reduction as soon as one earns an income fits well within that.

155649.explanation: CDA finds participation in society very important. If experience places can be a stepping stone to regular work, then their use is important.

155650.explanation: Preference is placement with regular employers. If that is not possible, people must not be left to fend for themselves. Social workplaces organized by the municipality can reintegrate people into society.

155651.explanation: Entrepreneurs are responsible for building their own business. That also involves investing in innovation. In many cases that pays off in the long term and the municipality has no role in that.

155652.explanation: Collection and expenses must be clearly linked. That creates support among entrepreneurs in the recreation and hospitality sectors for collecting the tourist tax.

155654.explanation: The nitrogen transition is the responsibility of the national government (Ministry of Agriculture, Nature and Food Quality). At local implementation, the municipality can assist. Part of this can be reimbursement of costs or other necessary support during the transition.

155655.explanation: CDA believes that young people must be more involved in politics and the municipal government. They are the future leaders of our municipality.

155656.explanation: The council is the elected representation of the people. Council members must ensure they are in contact with residents and gather voices from society. A citizens’ council can contribute to democracy if it focuses on a specific topic. This increases involvement.

155660.explanation: Integrating holiday parks requires a tailored approach. Size and location are important factors in making that choice. Refusing in advance has a negative effect on recreational growth within the municipality.

155661.explanation: National rules are followed in this. The municipality of Lochem has no experts in the field of agricultural chemicals. We depend on national regulations.

155662.explanation: CDA considers Easter fires a valuable tradition. The municipality will continue to explore how this can be done as sustainably as possible, for example by combining collections.

155664.explanation: The municipality can positively stimulate developments. Contributions apply to initial changes. The goal is that farmers and residents can manage independently afterward.

155665.explanation: Separated waste collection is already very successful. Residents should not be penalized by having to bring waste to disposal points. Bringing it can also cause environmental damage if people transport (larger) quantities by car to collection points.

155667.explanation: Information campaigns are a task of the national government, because this kind of fraud does not remain within municipal boundaries.

155668.explanation: The power grid must be reliably secured for the entire municipality. It cannot be divided down to the level of neighborhoods.

155669.explanation: There are guidelines and legislation for this. The military must be able to train to keep our country safe.

155671.explanation: Clear cycling routes within neighborhoods should make traveling by bike safer.

155672.explanation: All public parking spaces within the municipality are available for car-sharing vehicles. A shared car may be parked in a neighborhood or parking lot. An app can indicate the exact location.

155673.explanation: Traffic safety is important. Replacement trees can be planted in an alternative location.

155675.explanation: The costs are too high for the number of times it can be deployed. The citizen network that responds during resuscitation is an effective way to achieve low response times in acute emergency assistance.

155676.explanation: It is the municipality's responsibility (based on a duty of care within the framework of participation) to ensure sufficient public toilets, partly due to the aging population in our municipality. Hospitality and retail sectors can help by providing public toilets.

155677.explanation: Creativity should have space in our municipality. People who do not follow the rules and vandalize other places must be dealt with firmly.

155681.explanation: Associations should be able to receive subsidies for inclusive participation or events where new people are introduced or encouraged to join. Club life is an important part of society that fosters cohesion.

155620.explanation: Entrepreneurs keep Lochem lively. Targeted city marketing strengthens the business climate, creates jobs, and aligns with the DPL course to actively support entrepreneurship with a practical, future-oriented approach.

155621.explanation: DPL wants to provide space for entrepreneurship but does not automatically choose more business parks. Expansion is only possible if it carefully fits the landscape, accessibility, and the needs of local businesses.

155626.explanation: DPL believes housing for seasonal workers must be well arranged but does not see this as an absolute obligation on the company's own premises. Solutions must fit the environment, scale, and livability and require customization.

155627.explanation: DPL wants to handle greenery carefully but does not choose a rigid one-for-one rule. It is about quality, location, and coherence: replant where it contributes to livability, nature, and climate, allowing for customization.

155628.explanation: DPL wants to allow room for personal choices but also believes that health and living environment matter. Use can be permitted as long as nuisance and health risks for neighbors are limited and rules remain enforceable.

155629.explanation: DPL does not choose a general ban. Solar panels on farmland are possible if carefully integrated, temporary, with community support, and without unnecessary damage to the landscape and agricultural function. Customization over dogmas.

155632.explanation: DPL wants to make the station area safe and inviting. Targeted camera surveillance can help, as a supplement to good lighting, clear layout, and visible enforcement.

155633.explanation: DPL is against priority for specific professional groups. Allocation of social rental housing must be fair and explainable, with social or economic ties to Lochem allowed to count.

155634.explanation: DPL does not choose fixed quotas. Housing construction must ensure a balanced mix with emphasis on affordable homeownership, starters, and housing turnover. Needs vary per location and require customization, not a generic percentage.

155635.explanation: DPL wants to counter abuse and speculation but does not opt for an absolute owner-occupancy obligation. Instruments such as owner-occupancy obligations for new builds or affordable homes can be used selectively where needed, allowing for customization.

155638.explanation: DPL wants to make sustainable mobility practically possible. More charging points are needed to keep electric driving accessible, provided this is done in balance and does not come at the expense of accessibility and livability.

155639.explanation: DPL does not choose a general reduction to 30 km/h. Traffic safety requires customization: 30 km/h where it contributes to safety and livability, and higher speeds where roads are suitable and traffic flow is important.

155640.explanation: DPL wants to enable new forms of ownership for tenants. Buying a social rental home can contribute to affordability, housing turnover, and greater control for residents over their living place, provided it is carefully and responsibly arranged.

155641.explanation: DPL wants to invest in housing forms where elderly can live together, appropriately, and with care nearby. This increases living comfort, strengthens mutual connection, and creates flow in the housing market.

155642.explanation: DPL considers housing construction necessary. Tree felling may be unavoidable in this, provided it is done carefully with attention to replanting, greenery quality, and a good balance between housing and living environment.

155643.explanation: DPL wants more small, affordable rental and owner-occupied homes for starters, singles, and seniors. This aligns with current demand and helps improve movement in the housing market.

155644.explanation: DPL believes refugees with a residency permit should be decently housed but does not automatically opt for extra spending on temporary solutions. Solutions must be manageable, temporary, and explainable and should not come at the expense of other residents' housing opportunities.

155645.explanation: DPL sticks to the agreement of a maximum of 250 shelter places. Clear limits are needed for controllability, safety, and support from neighbors, with clear agreements and structural involvement of the community.

155647.explanation: DPL believes work must pay off. Automatic cuts when earning extra discourage people from (re)entering the workforce. The municipality should provide incentives that stimulate participation and exit from social assistance.

155649.explanation: DPL wants everyone who can participate to have the chance. Sheltered jobs and meaningful day activities provide structure, self-esteem, and social connection for people who are long-term unemployed.

155650.explanation: DPL wants to offer perspective to people with barriers to the labor market but does not see a new social employment facility as self-evident. Collaboration with existing organizations and customized guidance toward work or day activities is preferred.

155651.explanation: DPL wants to stimulate entrepreneurship and innovation. A fund for innovative ideas helps entrepreneurs progress and strengthens the local economy, provided it is practically organized and aimed at initiatives that actually work.

155652.explanation: DPL believes tourist tax should benefit tourism and recreation. Investing in cycling routes, facilities, and quality enhances visits and the local economy and is fair to visitors.

155654.explanation: DPL is against automatic subsidies for organic farming. Farmers must be free to operate; the transition should be economically feasible. Support can only be targeted, temporary, and innovation-focused, not as a standard flow of money.

155655.explanation: DPL finds it important that young people are involved in policies that affect them. A youth council increases participation, engagement, and understanding of local democracy, provided it is practical and seriously organized.

155656.explanation: DPL is open to resident involvement but does not see a randomly selected citizens' assembly as a standard instrument. Participation must be targeted, representative, and complementary to elected democracy, with a clear role and added value.

155660.explanation: DPL does not choose a general ban on new holiday parks. Developments can be allowed if carefully integrated, fit the landscape and livability, and demonstrably add to the local economy and tourism offer.

155661.explanation: DPL values health and living environment but does not automatically opt for stricter rules. Protection of neighbors and nature requires careful consideration and customization, balanced with a workable agricultural practice.

155662.explanation: DPL values local traditions such as Easter fires. Allowance can be given if done safely, well organized, and taking into account health, nature, and nuisance for residents.

155664.explanation: DPL wants to strengthen nature and landscape but does not opt for automatic subsidies. Support can be targeted and temporary if initiatives demonstrably contribute to nature quality, have support, and are practically feasible.

155665.explanation: DPL believes waste policy must work in practice. Paying per container increases the risk of additional waste and litter. A simpler and fairer system contributes better to a clean and livable environment.

155667.explanation: DPL wants residents to become more digitally resilient. Education and support against online scams and cybercrime increase safety, especially for the elderly and small entrepreneurs.

155668.explanation: DPL wants the basics to keep functioning during outages or crises. An emergency power supply per village increases self-reliance and safety, so that village halls can continue to operate as meeting and information points.

155669.explanation: DPL is open to cooperation with defense exercises if this contributes to safety and resilience. Nuisance must be taken seriously, but making structural objections is not appropriate; however, good agreements and clear communication with the surrounding area are necessary.

155671.explanation: DPL believes traffic rules must be clear and uniform everywhere. Automatic priority for cyclists does not fit in with this. Traffic safety requires good road design and customization, not exceptions in the rules.

155672.explanation: DPL wants to stimulate sustainable and efficient mobility. More parking spaces for shared cars make car sharing more attractive and can reduce parking pressure, provided this is done in a balanced way and fits local needs.

155673.explanation: DPL considers traffic safety essential. Trees that demonstrably pose a danger must be allowed to be cut down, after careful consideration and, where possible, replanting, so that safety and green spaces remain in balance.

155675.explanation: DPL considers fast and good ambulance care crucial, but does not see a municipal ambulance station as a given. Deployment must be effective and well-coordinated with regional care partners, so that quality and response times truly improve.

155676.explanation: DPL wants an accessible public space for everyone. More public toilets, especially in Lochem city and at busy locations, make the municipality more hospitable and better usable for the elderly, families, and people with disabilities.

155677.explanation: DPL sees street art as a form of expression. Legal places for graffiti prevent nuisance, give young people room for creativity, and contribute to a well-maintained and lively public space.

155681.explanation: DPL values culture highly for livability and social encounters. Local music and theater associations strengthen social cohesion and deserve sufficient support to remain accessible and vital.

statements.title: The municipality may only spend tourist tax revenue on tourism and recreation, such as scenic cycling routes.

titleParts.text: De gemeente mag geld uit de

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

titleParts.text: alleen uitgeven aan toerisme en recreatie, zoals mooie fietsroutes.

statements.moreInfo: Tourist tax is the charge that tourists pay for each overnight stay in a hotel or on a campsite. In Lochem a tourist in a hotel pays €2.80 per night. On a campsite a tourist pays €1.10 per night.
statements.pro: Tourists pay the tourist tax. Therefore it is fair to spend this money only on tourism.
statements.con: The municipality also needs the money from the tourist tax for other things, such as maintenance of public green spaces.

statements.title: More business parks should be developed in Lochem.
statements.moreInfo: A business park is an area for factories, distribution companies and offices. The municipality determines where such an area will be located. In the municipality of Lochem there are now ten business parks. Two of them are currently being developed.
statements.pro: Business parks provide more companies and more jobs in the municipality. The municipality of Lochem must give businesses space so they do not relocate to other municipalities. There is too little room on the existing business parks to grow.
statements.con: More business parks come at the expense of nature. The existing business parks can still expand. There is too little space in the municipality for any more business parks.

statements.title: The municipality of Lochem should continue to allow the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves.

titleParts.text: De gemeente Lochem moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.moreInfo: Municipalities can make their own rules for the use of wood-burning stoves and open fireplaces.
statements.pro: In the municipality of Lochem, open fireplaces and wood stoves are used a lot. The municipality can inform residents about responsible burning. Therefore banning open fireplaces and wood stoves is not necessary.
statements.con: Open fireplaces and wood stoves are bad for the environment. Open fireplaces and wood stoves are bad for health.

statements.title: At least 35% of newly built homes in Lochem must be social rental housing. In Lochem, 17% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Lochem moet minimaal 35% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Lochem is nu 17% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.moreInfo: For a social rental home you pay lower rent. They are usually owned by a housing corporation. Only people with a lower income can get such a home. Many people want such a home. The waiting list is long.
statements.pro: Social rental housing is important for people with little money.
statements.con: Young starters in the municipality of Lochem do not need social rental housing, but primarily affordable homes to buy.

statements.title: The municipality should invest in a promotional campaign for Lochem as an attractive location for businesses.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in een

titleParts.glossary: A series of advertisements on, for example, billboards, television, and social media.
The location where a company establishes itself.

statements.moreInfo: Through a promotional campaign the municipality can show companies why it is attractive to locate in Lochem. This can be done through advertisements, social media or a website.
statements.pro: There will be more jobs in the municipality if there are more businesses.
statements.con: If more companies arrive, more green space will disappear and the municipality of Lochem will become a less pleasant place to live.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality of Lochem has rules for small solar parks. For example, the landscape must not be damaged. The municipality is temporarily not granting permission for large solar parks because there is no capacity on the electricity grid.
statements.pro: Solar panels can also be placed on roofs. Agricultural land should remain agricultural so there is enough food.
statements.con: Small solar parks that do not cause nuisance should be allowed to remain. The municipality should assess for each location whether solar panels fit. There is no reason to introduce a ban.

statements.title: Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves.
statements.moreInfo: Municipalities can impose an owner-occupancy requirement on buyers of homes. This means buyers must live in the house they buy for a certain period, usually between 2 and 5 years. During that period they may not rent out or sell the house.
statements.pro: Private landlords buy homes to make a profit. They charge high rents, which makes housing more expensive. If buyers of new houses have to live in them themselves, homes will end up with Lochem residents.
statements.con: Be able to buy a home where their child can live. When people buy a house, they may decide for themselves who lives in it.

statements.title: Lochem should invest in a new sheltered employment facility.
statements.moreInfo: A sheltered workplace is a place where people with a physical, intellectual or psychological disability can work. Until 2015 many municipalities had such facilities. The national government decided that people with disabilities could work in regular companies with support. Since then almost all sheltered workplaces have closed, including in the municipality of Lochem.
statements.pro: People with a disability cannot always work for regular employers and need extra guidance. A sheltered workshop is a step toward a regular job.
statements.con: The municipality of Lochem does not need to have a sheltered workshop. This can be arranged with other nearby municipalities. People with disabilities can also work for regular employers.

statements.title: The municipality should spend more on temporary housing for refugees with residence permits, for example in container housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld steken in tijdelijke huisvesting voor vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
Moveable and temporary homes, usually made from a shipping container.

statements.moreInfo: The municipality of Lochem is required by the national government to offer housing to refugees with a residence permit. In 2025 thirty-four people found housing; forty-two refugees with a residence permit are still waiting for housing in the municipality of Lochem.
statements.pro: Temporary housing for refugees can later also be used by residents. A few years ago the municipality built temporary housing for refugees from Ukraine. That was a success.
statements.con: The municipality should not build or pay for housing but leave that to companies. There are already enough reception places in the municipality for refugees with a residence permit. The municipality of Lochem should not spend money on temporary housing for refugees with a residence permit; that should be done by the national government.

statements.title: The municipality should invest in new housing where older people can live together and receive care.
statements.moreInfo: Residential places where elderly people can receive care include, for example, courtyards where older people can live independently with care nearby. In Lochem about one in three residents is older than 65, and 14% of residents are older than 75. The number of elderly people will increase in the coming years.
statements.pro: More and more elderly people live in the municipality of Lochem. Therefore the municipality must invest in housing options for the elderly. When elderly people move to senior housing, their homes become available to young families.
statements.con: The municipality does not need to fund housing where older people can live together and receive care. The municipality can leave this to companies that build homes. If there is enough demand, they will naturally build housing for older people.
