statements.title: The municipality of Lochem should provide more public toilets.
statements.moreInfo: In the municipality of Lochem there are no public toilets in public places such as squares, parks or bus stops. Through the Hoge Nood App people can, however, use 22 opened toilets in cafés, shops or museums.
statements.pro: Sufficient toilets make it easier, especially for elderly people, to go out.
statements.con: There are sufficient toilets at businesses, such as cafés and restaurants.

statements.title: The municipality should offer sheltered jobs to people who have been unemployed long-term; they could, for example, pick up litter or have coffee with lonely people.
statements.moreInfo: Basic jobs are positions the municipality creates itself for people who find it difficult to get a job. They are often local jobs. In the municipality of Lochem 2.4% of households live on social assistance. That is 370 households.
statements.pro: Work gives long-term unemployed people self-confidence. Therefore the municipality must organise work for them.
statements.con: The municipality of Lochem does enough to help people who are long-term unemployed find a job.

statements.title: Lochem should form a citizens' assembly by lottery to advise the municipality.

titleParts.text: Lochem moet met een loting een

titleParts.glossary: A group of residents chosen by lot that advises politicians or helps decide on a specific issue.

statements.moreInfo: A citizens’ assembly is a group of residents that assists the municipal council with important decisions. Residents cannot sign up themselves; the municipality selects participants by lottery and sends a letter if they may take part. This aims to include as many different kinds of people as possible. The citizens’ assembly receives advice from independent experts, but can also invite interest groups. Participants discuss the issues and eventually give advice to the municipality. The municipality is not obliged to adopt this advice.
statements.pro: A citizens' assembly increases residents' involvement in important administrative decisions. A citizens' assembly often produces nice and unexpected ideas and solutions.
statements.con: The elected municipal council already speaks for the residents of Lochem. A citizens' assembly is therefore not necessary. In the municipality of Lochem, residents can already have sufficient input when the municipality makes important decisions.

statements.title: No new holiday parks should be allowed in the municipality of Lochem.
statements.moreInfo: In the municipality of Lochem there are four large parks with more than one hundred holiday homes. In addition, there are eight smaller parks with fewer than fifty holiday homes or static caravans.
statements.pro: The arrival of even more holiday parks comes at the expense of nature and livability.
statements.con: Tourism is important for the economy of the municipality of Lochem. New holiday parks are needed to support that.

statements.title: Lochem should establish a youth council to advise the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: A group of young people who provide input and discuss plans and policy with the municipality.

statements.moreInfo: A youth council is a group of young people who advise the municipality. Young people aged 18 to 25 can contribute ideas to the municipality. The youth council may also propose its own initiatives. The municipality is not obliged to adopt the advice.
statements.pro: Young people have new ideas and a fresh perspective. Through a youth council these ideas reach the municipality. A youth council involves young people more in municipal politics.
statements.con: The municipal council speaks on behalf of all residents, including young people. Therefore a youth council is not necessary. The municipality of Lochem had a youth council a few years ago; it did not work well and was discontinued.

statements.title: The municipality should invest in its own ambulance station.
statements.moreInfo: The Safety Region is a cooperative partnership between municipalities that provides fire services, crisis management and ambulance care. The municipality can provide input on response times and safety, and can offer a location for an ambulance.
statements.pro: The ambulance now comes from Zutphen, Apeldoorn, and Deventer. As a result it sometimes takes a long time before help arrives in the municipality of Lochem. Therefore a local ambulance base is needed.
statements.con: A permanent pitch is too expensive.

statements.title: The municipality should create stricter rules for the use of (agricultural) pesticides around schools, homes and nature areas.
statements.moreInfo: National rules apply to the use of (agricultural) pesticides. Still, the municipality can take additional measures. For example, the municipality can establish a spray-free zone around new homes-an area of about 50 metres around the house where pesticides may not be applied.
statements.pro: As long as agricultural chemicals are not banned nationally, the municipality of Lochem must intervene itself. Agricultural chemicals are harmful to health. Therefore stricter rules are needed for use near schools, homes, and nature areas.
statements.con: There are already national rules on the use of pesticides. Additional municipal rules are not necessary. The municipality of Lochem does not have in-house expertise on pesticides.

statements.title: The municipality of Lochem should continue to allow Easter fires.
statements.moreInfo: The Easter fires in the municipality of Lochem take place each year on Easter Sunday. They occur at multiple locations and are organized by various organizations. In 2025 the Easter fires almost did not go ahead because the drought made the risk of wildfires seem high. In the end the municipality allowed the Easter fires to proceed.
statements.pro: Easter bonfires are a tradition in the municipality of Lochem and are part of the local culture.
statements.con: Easter fires are bad for air quality.

statements.title: The municipality should stop the system where residents pay per container for their waste.
statements.moreInfo: In the municipality of Lochem each household pays the fixed waste charge of €117.18. In addition, residents pay per container. A container for vegetable, fruit and garden waste (VFG/compostable waste) costs €2.87 per container. Residual waste costs €10.10 per container.
statements.pro: Residents pay per container in Lochem. To save costs they more often dump waste illegally. That must stop.
statements.con: Paying per waste container encourages residents to sort their waste better.

statements.title: The municipality should designate legal spaces for graffiti and street art.
statements.moreInfo: Graffiti is visual language often sprayed illegally on walls with spray cans. Street art is an art form that ranges from murals to stickers. An example of street art is the mural on the side walls of the storage silos on the Goorseweg in Lochem. This artwork is 1,300 square metres in size and is thus one of the largest pieces of street art in the Netherlands.
statements.pro: The municipality of Lochem should provide more space for creativity. The nuisance is reduced if there are legal places for graffiti. Graffiti and street art make Lochem’s public space more vibrant.
statements.con: It is forbidden to damage buildings, even if it is presented as art. The nuisance does not stop if legal places for graffiti are created. Graffiti can also be done on the grounds of youth clubs and other creative centers; it does not have to be in public space.

statements.title: Each village should have its own emergency power supply.
statements.moreInfo: An emergency power supply is a system that provides electricity when the normal network fails.
statements.pro: In emergencies, such as a storm, power is important. Therefore the municipality should fund emergency power supplies. Emergency power supplies make residents of Lochem less dependent on regional or national assistance.
statements.con: It costs too much to provide each village with its own emergency power supply. Power supply is a task of the province. Therefore the province should provide emergency power supplies.

statements.title: No more than 250 people should ever be accommodated in the asylum seekers' centre (AZC).

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

titleParts.text: (azc) mogen nooit meer dan 250 mensen worden opgevangen.

statements.moreInfo: In 2025 an asylum seekers’ centre (azc) opened on the Ampenseweg in Lochem. The municipal council decided that up to 250 asylum seekers will be accommodated there. There is a large shortage of reception places across the Netherlands.
statements.pro: The Lochem municipal council decided at the end of 2025 that a maximum of 250 people may be accommodated in the asylum seekers centre. The municipality must adhere to that agreement. Large asylum seekers centres cause nuisance.
statements.con: The municipality must shelter people fleeing war and violence. The Lochem municipal council decided at the end of 2025 that 250 people would be accommodated in the asylum seekers' center. But additional accommodation must be possible.

statements.title: The municipality should object to low-flying military helicopters from the Het Groote Veld training area.
statements.moreInfo: The Ministry of Defence has designated areas across the Netherlands where military personnel can train. Low-flying helicopters will be trained from the Het Groote Veld site.
statements.pro: Low-flying helicopters cause nuisance.
statements.con: Soldiers need a place to train to keep our country safe.

statements.title: Cyclists should have priority over cars in villages and neighbourhoods.
statements.moreInfo: A bicycle street is a road where cyclists have priority. Such a street often has red asphalt and a sign reading “cars are guests.” Cars may drive at a maximum of 30 kilometres per hour and must not impede cyclists.
statements.pro: Streets do not become safer if cyclists are given priority over cars in some places. That causes confusion.
statements.con: Streets where cyclists always have priority over cars are safer.

statements.title: The municipality should help residents protect themselves against online dangers, such as digital scams.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces for car-sharing vehicles.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer parkeerplaatsen aanwijzen voor

titleParts.glossary: Cars that multiple people can use.

statements.moreInfo: A car-sharing vehicle is a car used by multiple people. People often rent them through companies and pay per day or per driven kilometre. Users can find the cars via an app or a special parking space reserved for car-shares. In the municipality of Lochem there are currently eight car-sharing vehicles parked in such a reserved spot. Other cars are not allowed to park there.
statements.pro: Fixed parking spots make the use of shared cars easier.
statements.con: Car-sharing vehicles can park in normal public parking spaces.

statements.title: When allocating social rental housing, teachers, nurses and police officers should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten leraren, verpleegkundigen en politieagenten voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should cut down trees that pose a danger to traffic.
statements.moreInfo: Trees along roads and paths in the countryside are on average 60 years old. Long branches that grow over the road are dangerous for traffic. The municipality therefore prunes one fifth of the ten thousand avenue trees on its territory each year. If a tree itself creates unsafe situations, the tree is felled.
statements.pro: Traffic safety is more important than protecting trees. For a felled tree another tree can be planted in a safer location.
statements.con: Trees are important for nature and livability. Therefore they must be protected. Lowering the speed limit makes traffic safer than cutting down trees.

statements.title: The municipality should invest in a fund for innovative ideas from entrepreneurs.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in een

titleParts.glossary: Money set aside for a specific purpose, commonly called a "pot."

shootoutStatements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.
There should be more camera surveillance in the Lochem station area.
The municipality may cut down trees to build new houses.
Tenants should be able to buy their social rental home.
More small rental and owner-occupied apartments should be built in Lochem.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
People on social assistance who earn extra money should have that benefit reduced. That should remain the case.
Local music and theater associations should receive more funding from the municipality.
For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
The municipality should give farmers money when they switch to organic farming.
The municipality should give money to farmers and residents who improve nature.
